records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Perplexed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Perplexing.]

defs: To involve; to entangle; to make intricate or complicated, and difficult to be unraveled or understood; as, to perplex one with doubts.
quotes: No artful wildness to perplex the scene.
What was thought obscure, perplexed, and too hard for our weak parts, will lie open to the understanding in a fair view.

defs: To embarrass; to puzzle; to distract; to bewilder; to confuse; to trouble with ambiguity, suspense, or anxiety.
quotes: We are perplexed, but not in despair.
We can distinguish no general truths, or at least shall be apt to perplex the mind.

defs: To plague; to vex; to torment.

records.ety: [L. perplexus entangled, intricate; per + plectere, plexum, to plait, braid: cf. F. perplexe. See Per-, and Plait.]
